error.missingRedirectUrl: Current redirect URL: not configured. Supabase may redirect to the project Site URL instead.

auth.loadingCopy: Checking whether a real Supabase session is available for setup persistence.

authenticated.copy: Your setup can now reload on this device because Behavior Pattern and Anchor Note save to your signed-in Supabase account.
authenticated.safety: Flare is a self-support tool. It does not monitor you in real time or contact anyone for you.

clientUnavailable.copy: Setup persistence is local-only because the public Supabase URL and anon key are not loaded into the Expo runtime.
clientUnavailable.safety: Flare does not monitor urgent risk or provide emergency response.

noSession.copy: Setup saves still work locally in memory, but they will not reload from Supabase until you authenticate.
noSession.safety: Flare is for self-support and reflection. It is not a crisis service, therapy replacement, or medical treatment.

modes.existingAccountDetail: Sign in is for an existing Supabase account on this project.
modes.firstTimeSetupDetail: Create account is the first-time setup path for a new Supabase user.

noSession.passwordHint: Password sign-in uses the current Supabase project settings. Magic link requires the configured redirect URL to be allowed in Supabase.
noSession.redirectMagicLinkPrefix: Magic link sent if email auth is enabled. It should return to
noSession.redirectMagicLinkFallback: Magic link sent if email auth is enabled. Without EXPO_PUBLIC_FLARE_AUTH_REDIRECT_URL, Supabase falls back to the project Site URL.
noSession.signUpNotice: Account created if sign-up is enabled. Check your email if confirmation is required.

setup.title: Customize what helps you pause and reflect
setup.subtitle: Keep your personal setup separate from the in-the-moment flow.

behaviorPattern.cardCopy: Define the pattern, likely triggers, and the next step you want handy when you are trying to interrupt an urge.
behaviorPattern.modalSubtitle: Keep it concrete enough to interrupt the pattern, but light enough to update anytime.
behaviorPattern.intro: Capture the pattern you want Flare to help you interrupt and reflect on. This stays editable in V0.
behaviorPattern.helperCopy: Save requires a behavior name and at least one next step.
behaviorPattern.summaryEmptyCopy: Add one lightweight pattern so Flare can show that your setup is ready.

shortDescription.label: Short description
shortDescription.placeholder: What the spiral feels like when it starts.

commonTriggers.label: Common triggers
commonTriggers.placeholder: Stress, boredom, being alone, an argument.

riskTimesOrSituations.label: Risk times or situations
riskTimesOrSituations.placeholder: Late nights, payday, after a setback.

preferredRecoveryActions.label: Preferred next steps
preferredRecoveryActions.placeholder: Put the phone down, stand up, drink water, step outside.

anchorNote.cardCopy: Capture grounded reminders, costs, and phrases you want Flare to show back to you during a flare.
anchorNote.modalSubtitle: Capture grounded words you want available when you are trying to pause.
anchorNote.intro: Keep it honest, specific, and easy to revisit. This note is for self-support, not treatment.
anchorNote.helperCopy: Save requires one reason to pause and one supportive phrase.
anchorNote.summaryEmptyCopy: Save a few grounded reminders so Flare Response can show them immediately during a flare.

interruptionReasons.label: Why interrupt this pattern?
interruptionReasons.placeholder: I want to stop before I lose the rest of tonight.

continuingCosts.label: Costs or consequences of continuing
continuingCosts.placeholder: I will feel foggy tomorrow and break trust with myself again.

groundedReminders.label: Reminder from grounded self
groundedReminders.placeholder: This urge peaks fast. You do not need to obey it.

emergencyActions.label: Immediate action or commitment
emergencyActions.placeholder: Put the phone in the kitchen, drink water, and walk outside.

supportivePhrase.label: Supportive phrase to show during a flare

telegram.title: Telegram Support
telegram.copy: Visible here as future-scoped direction only. V0 does not send messages, alerts, or outreach on your behalf.

flare.title: Use Flare to pause, regroup, and choose a next step
flare.subtitle: Keep the flow simple: one main action, one secondary reflection entry, and lightweight readiness cues.

sendButton.label: Send Flare
sendButton.hint: Starts a new Flare Event and opens Flare Response right away.
sendButton.copy: Mark this moment, open your saved support copy, and pick a next step with no extra confirmation screen.

secondaryAction.label: Checkpoint / Reflection
secondaryAction.copy: Open a lightweight reflection sheet without leaving this screen.

readiness.title: Readiness
readiness.localOnlyUntilConfigLoaded: Local-only until public Supabase config is loaded

responseModal.title: Flare Response
responseModal.subtitle: This V0 sheet opens immediately and stays attached to the current Flare Event.

response.defaultInterruptionReason: Flare shows your saved Anchor Note right after Send Flare so your own words are close at hand.
response.safety: Flare is a self-support tool. If you may hurt yourself or someone else, or you need immediate help, contact local emergency services or a crisis hotline now.
response.defaultNextStep: Step away from the trigger for two minutes, drink water, and give yourself a short pause before choosing what comes next.

checkpoint.modalSubtitle: Keep the reflection lightweight and attach it to the current event.
checkpoint.helperCopy: Save requires an active event plus the main reflection fields.

context.activeCopy: This reflection will update the event you started from Send Flare.
context.missingCopy: Send Flare first to create the current event, then save a brief reflection here.

checkpoint.intro: Use this space to notice what happened and what helped. Flare does not provide therapy, medical advice, or crisis response.

whatHappened.label: What happened?
whatHappened.placeholder: I hit the urge after getting home and started pacing.

whatHelped.label: What helped?
whatHelped.placeholder: I stepped outside and texted a friend instead.

howIFeelNow.label: How do I feel now?
howIFeelNow.placeholder: Still activated, but less locked in.

outcome.label: Outcome
outcome.placeholder: Urge dropped enough to keep moving.

note.label: Optional note
note.placeholder: The first two minutes were the hardest.

history.title: Review past flares without turning this into analytics
history.subtitle: A lightweight chronological list of past Flare Events and attached Checkpoint / Reflection notes.
history.intro: Signed-in sessions load your own saved event history. Signed-out sessions stay local-only. Flare does not monitor you in real time or score your behavior.

search.title: No matching Flare Events
search.copy: Try a different search term from the behavior snapshot or reflection notes.

archived.title: No archived Flare Events
archived.copy: Archive keeps older events out of the default view without deleting them.

reflected.title: No reflected Flare Events
reflected.copy: Checkpoint / Reflection entries appear here after you save a reflection.

default.title: No Flare Events yet
default.copy: Send Flare to log the first event. Signed-in sessions can reload saved events here, while signed-out sessions stay local-only.

detailModal.title: Flare Event details
detailModal.subtitle: Review the saved behavior snapshot and any attached reflection before deciding whether to archive or restore it.
